title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class: A100 at 20% (120/312), H200 at 21% (47/187), B200 at 14% (87/176), and H100 in severe shortage at 10% (34/182). H100 spot surged 42.9% day-over-day to $2.86/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, signaling acute demand pressure likely driven by H100 utilization hitting 90.3%, up 10.5 points in 30 days.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class: A100 at 20% (120/312), H200 at 21% (47/187), B200 at 14% (87/176), and H100 in severe shortage at 10% (34/182). H100 spot surged 42.9% day-over-day to $2.86/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, signaling acute demand pressure likely driven by H100 utilization hitting 90.3%, up 10.5 points in 30 days.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $10.45/M tokens, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling at catastrophic losses exceeding $10/M tokens. Grades: OpenAI Strong (premium tiers print 89-97% margins on B200), Anthropic Neutral (Opus reprices high but Haiku compresses), Google Neutral (Flash at $2.50 is lean), DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 is undervalued at $5.00/hr spot with 14% availability, the best margin profile in the stack (92-98% at premium/ultra tiers), and tightening supply makes it the long trade. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued given GPT-5.4 matches frontier capability at $15/M, a 5.3x premium begging for substitution.
bullets: 10% of H100 GPUs are available, the tightest supply of any class with only 34 of 182 units free.
14% B200 availability across 176 GPUs marks severe shortage territory heading into summer training season.
$2.86/hr H100 spot represents a 42.9% single-day price spike, the largest move across all GPU classes.
121.7% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable compute contract to hold.
$0.06/M output for Llama 3.1 8B and Gemma 2 2B sets the open-source floor, a 99.9% decline from GPT-4 launch pricing.
21.76 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token costs have fallen 78% since March 2023 baseline.
